free_text: Rearrangement of valence neutrons in the neutrinoless
University of the West of Scotland, Paisley
Insitut de Physique Nucleaire d Orsay
Enriched barium oxide targets of thickness 50-75
ug/cm**2, mounted on 40 ug/cm**2 thick carbon backings
136Ba and 134Ba were determined in order to probe the
neutron vacancy and occupancy. In case of the (d,p)
and (p,d) reactions, only relative yields for the
different states could be determined due to target
uncertainty due to differences between targets
statistical uncertainty of the exp. data
Experimental results were compared to DWBA
Close in energy to another state at 3190 keV
No state reported in the literature previously
at this energy.  Energy is rounded to the
